content: Human linguistic reflection (the lexical entry of 'reflexion' taught in high school English curricula) encodes implicit scaffolding for effective LLM self-reflection that AI researchers have not yet fully leveraged. The dual meaning of reflexion as both 'light/information reflection' and 'deliberate self-evaluation' maps directly to the missing multi-perspective consistency check that modern LLM self-reflection methods like Self-Contrast and Mirror aim to implement.
